prompt: What happens if a `std::thread` object is destroyed while still joinable?

options.text: It auto-joins on destruction (and supports cooperative stop)
It provides mutual exclusion for critical sections
It orders memory like atomic_seq_cst always
options.explanation: Mutexes provide synchronization but not like that statement.

options.text: Locks on construction, unlocks on destruction

prompt: Which atomic ordering is the strongest (most restrictive)?
What is a data race in C++?

options.text: Two threads read the same variable
Two threads access same memory concurrently, at least one is a write, without synchronization
A race condition always causes UB
options.explanation: Race condition is logic; data race specifically causes UB.
